summary: Malicious npm package exfiltrates console output to attacker Telegram bot and Firebase
details: Package 0e@0.0.1 installs a global console Proxy on import that intercepts all console method calls and exfiltrates their arguments to a hardcoded Telegram bot (token 1998266306:AAEiVerla7_-JjR2fNc539ahJsGZg2xlpwY) and a Firebase endpoint (iiilll.firebaseio.com), as confirmed by and corroborated by the a static rule a static pattern match (api.telegram.org/bot + sendMessage + chat_id in src/6cc.js:36). The hardcoded Telegram bot token is attacker-controlled C2 infrastructure. The package is a disposable-name, v0.0.1, no-description package whose only behavior is covert interception and exfiltration of caller data on import. Three independent block signals converge: covert global monkey-patching, hardcoded exfiltration credential, and attacker-controlled outbound traffic on import.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
